original_hypothesis.title: Infinite Token Minting via Tax Rate Underflow
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: If an admin sets the tax rate above 100%, the unchecked arithmetic in tax processing will underflow, minting near-infinite tokens to the recipient.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: In `AgentToken.sol`, `setProjectTaxRates` does not enforce an upper bound on `projectBuyTaxBasisPoints` or `projectSellTaxBasisPoints`. If an admin (or compromised admin key) sets the rate > 10,000 (100%), the calculated `tax` in `_taxProcessing` will be greater than `sentAmount_`. Because the subtraction `amountLessTax_ -= tax` occurs inside an `unchecked` block, it will underflow, resulting in `amountLessTax_` becoming a massive number (~uint256.max). This massive number is then added to the recipient's balance, effectively minting infinite tokens.

proof_of_signal.pattern: amountLessTax_ -= tax inside unchecked block with no upper bound on tax rate

preconditions: Admin or factory sets tax rate > 10000 basis points
A transfer occurs that triggers the tax

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Underflow in unchecked block causes massive balance increase
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Recipient gains near-infinite tokens, which can be dumped to drain the LP

economic_questions: Is there any off-chain safeguard preventing the admin from setting high tax rates?
validation_requirements: Simulate setting tax to 10001 and performing a transfer to verify underflow

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Complete destruction of the token economy

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All token holders and LP providers

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check admin key security and timelocks
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify unchecked block behavior in Solidity 0.8+

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
